differences.title: 1. DATA TYPES
differences.leftDesc: Supports strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, and more.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily stores strings associated with keys.

differences.title: 2. PERSISTENCE
differences.leftDesc: Can save data to disk for durability (RDB, AOF).
differences.rightDesc: Data is lost on restart; purely ephemeral.

differences.title: 3. ATOMIC OPERATIONS
differences.leftDesc: Many commands operate atomically, ensuring data integrity.

differences.title: 4. COMPLEX QUERIES
differences.leftDesc: Supports pub/sub, Lua scripting, geospatial queries.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily for GET and SET operations.

differences.title: 5. MEMORY MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Flexible eviction policies, but can be more memory-intensive.
differences.rightDesc: LRU (Least Recently Used) eviction is standard.

differences.title: 6. COMMUNITY & ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Broader community, more integrations, extensive documentation.
differences.rightDesc: Mature and widely adopted for caching.

differences.title: 7. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Caching, session stores, message brokers, real-time apps.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily accelerating web applications and APIs.
